message:
fix(mvcc): validate WAL invariants across checkpoint-covered prefix

Reorder WAL replay record handling so D1 (strict commit_seq monotonicity)
and D8 (sentinel rejection) are enforced on every decoded record, rather
than skipped along with apply for commits already covered by the
checkpoint. Previously, a corrupted prefix below skip_up_to_seq could
slip past validation and cause the replay to be reported Clean even when
the on-disk log was malformed.

Changes in crates/ffs-mvcc/src/wal_replay.rs:

- Track last_decoded_seq separately from last_replayed_seq so the
  monotonicity check sees the actual decoded order, not the post-skip
  apply order.
- Move the D8 sentinel check ahead of the checkpoint skip so
  u64::MAX commit_seq / txn_id values are rejected unconditionally,
  even when they fall inside the covered prefix.
- Move the D1 monotonicity check ahead of the checkpoint skip and run
  it against last_decoded_seq, so a malformed prefix with non-monotonic
  seqs still produces ReplayOutcome::MonotonicityViolation (or fails
  fast under TailPolicy::FailFast) instead of being silently elided.
- Only apply the checkpoint skip after both invariants have been
  validated, preserving the existing apply semantics for well-formed
  logs.

Tests added in the same module:

- replay_validates_monotonicity_inside_skipped_checkpoint_prefix:
  encodes seqs [1,3,2,4] with skip_up_to_seq=3 and asserts the engine
  surfaces MonotonicityViolation { violating_seq: 2, expected_after: 3 }
  with no commits applied.
- replay_fail_fast_rejects_non_monotonic_skipped_prefix: same shape
  under TailPolicy::FailFast and asserts the error message contains
  "monotonicity" and "FailFast", and that the apply closure was never
  invoked.
- replay_rejects_sentinel_even_when_checkpoint_would_skip_it: encodes a
  single u64::MAX-seq record with skip_up_to_seq=u64::MAX and asserts
  CorruptTail { records_discarded: 1, first_corrupt_offset: 0 } with
  applied=false.
- replay_empty_log: replaced the panicking apply closure with an
  observable flag and asserts apply was never invoked, matching the
  pattern used by the new tests and removing a brittle panic-as-oracle.

Net effect: WAL replay can no longer return ReplayOutcome::Clean for a
prefix containing duplicate, decreasing, or sentinel commit_seq values,
closing a recovery-correctness gap in the checkpoint-skip path.
